analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (suggested direction.txt): counterparty-conditioned forward mids on Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 trades days 1–3 joined to same-timestamp price rows. Horizons K∈{5,20,100} map to +K*100 timestamp units. Aggression: trade price vs concurrent L1 bid/ask. Spread regime: per-product tertiles on pooled tape. Script: r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended below (round4_phase1_complete). Phase 2 not started until sim/backtest validates top edges under worse fills.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Strongest cell: Mark 67 aggressive buys on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT show large positive self-mid change at k=5 (n=164, mean≈1.96 ticks, t≈13) stable in tight/all bins; Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 show negative k=5 self-mid (n=160, mean≈-0.20, t≈-3.3). Mark 55 on extract is horizon-sensitive (buy k=5/20 positive mean; sell k=100 negative).

phase1_bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair cell means + residuals)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Pair-conditioned means at k=20 are tabulated; residual file ranks pairs after subtracting global pair mean — dominant structure remains Mark 01→Mark 22 mass on vouchers (see initial summary), residuals highlight smaller sub-pairs for Phase-2 conditioning.

Directed edge table matches repo sketch (Mark 01→Mark 22 top count). Two-hop chains within 5k timestamp units show repeated Mark 38→Mark 14→Mark 38 with mildly negative extract forward mean (n=885) vs rare high-n tails (e.g. Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 22 n=19 only).
387 multi-print timestamps; extract forward Δmid (k=20) mean≈0.33 (n=1608) on burst rows vs ≈0.08 (n=2671) off burst — bursts align with larger short-horizon extract drift in-sample.

When Mark 22 is aggressive seller, subsequent k=20 self-mid markout for the traded symbol is worst for prints with Mark 49 as buyer (n=12, very negative mean) and mildest for Mark 01 basket flow (n=1339 near zero) — candidate avoid-quote / fade tail vs Mark 49.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Follow Mark 67 aggressive buy prints on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT with short-horizon long bias (or avoid fading) at k=5
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean Δmid ≈ +1.96 price units at k=5 on same symbol

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Treat Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 as short-term bearish for 5300 mid (k=5)
Use same-timestamp multi-symbol bursts as extract risk-on / drift indicator (forward extract k=20)
Mark 55 on extract: separate buy vs sell horizons — aggressive buys mildly positive k=5/20; aggressive sells strongly negative k=100
Two-hop motif Mark 55→Mark 01→Mark 22 predicts negative extract forward (k=20) in bulk (n=535)

no_edge_or_negative_results: Mark 01 aggressive buy specifically on VEV_5300 at k=20: zero qualifying rows in tape slice (Mark 01→Mark 22 basket hits other lines); r4_p1_mark01_buy_vev5300_by_day_k20.csv empty — cannot claim participant alpha on that exact cell.
High-|t| two-hop tails (e.g. Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 22) have n<20 — not stable as standalone rules.
Phase 1 is mid-only markouts; tradable PnL under worse fills not established (STRATEGY caveat applies).
